data.text: I'll read the current CI configuration and Makefile before making changes.

data.output:
1	name: CI
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [ main, develop ]
6	  pull_request:
7	    branches: [ main ]
8	
9	# Minimal permissions by default
10	permissions:
11	  contents: read
12	  checks: write
13	  pull-requests: write
14	
15	jobs:
16	  # Main CI job using lefthook
17	  ci:
18	    name: CI Validation
19	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
20	    steps:
21	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
22	      with:
23	        fetch-depth: 0  # Need full history for some checks
24	    
25	    - name: Set up Go
26	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
27	      with:
28	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
29	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
30	
31	    - name: golangci-lint
32	      uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v8
33	      with:
34	        version: v2.11.4
35	        working-directory: cli
36	        skip-cache: false
37	
38	    - name: Install lefthook
39	      run: |
40	        # Install using Go for better reliability in CI
41	        go install github.com/evilmartians/lefthook@latest
42	        echo "$(go env GOPATH)/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
43	
44	    - name: Verify git-subtree availability
45	      run: |
46	        # git-subtree should be included with git, verify it's available
47	        if ! git subtree --help >/dev/null 2>&1; then
48	          echo "git-subtree not found, attempting to install..."
49	          # On some distros it's a separate package
50	          sudo apt-get update
51	          sudo apt-get install -y git-subtree || true
52	          # Verify again
53	          if ! git subtree --help >/dev/null 2>&1; then
54	            echo "ERROR: git-subtree is not available"
55	            exit 1
56	          fi
57	        fi
58	        echo "✓ git-subtree is available"
59	
60	    - name: Set up Bun
61	      uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
62	      with:
63	        bun-version: latest
64	
65	    - name: Install root dependencies
66	      run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
67	
68	    - name: Lint DESIGN.md
69	      run: bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md
70	
71	    - name: Build CLI binary
72	      run: |
73	        cd cli
74	        make build
75	
76	    - name: Validate bead schema
77	      run: make bead-schema
78	
79	    - name: Validate skill metadata
80	      run: make skill-schema
81	
82	    - name: Validate ddx skill (agentskills.io conformance)
83	      run: scripts/eval-skill.sh --validate
84	
85	    - name: Run CI validation
86	      run: lefthook run ci
87	      env:
88	        LEFTHOOK_QUIET: meta,execution
89	
90	    - name: Guard server state against test pollution
91	      run: |
92	        set -euo pipefail
93	
94	        export XDG_DATA_HOME="$(mktemp -d)"
95	
96	        cd cli
97	        go test ./... -count=1
98	
99	        ./build/ddx server --addr 127.0.0.1 --port 17743 --tsnet=false > ../ddx-state-guard-server.log 2>&1 &
100	        server_pid=$!
101	        cleanup() {
102	          kill "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
103	          wait "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
104	        }
105	        trap cleanup EXIT
106	
107	        state_file="$XDG_DATA_HOME/ddx/server-state.json"
108	        for _ in $(seq 1 20); do
109	          if [ -f "$state_file" ]; then
110	            break
111	          fi
112	          sleep 0.25
113	        done
114	
115	        if [ ! -f "$state_file" ]; then
116	          cat ../ddx-state-guard-server.log
117	          echo "server-state.json was not created"
118	          exit 1
119	        fi
120	
121	        project_count="$(jq '.projects | length' "$state_file")"
122	        if [ "$project_count" -gt 20 ]; then
123	          jq '.projects[] | {name, path}' "$state_file"
124	          echo "server-state.json contains $project_count projects; expected <= 20"
125	          exit 1
126	        fi
127	
128	        if jq -e '.projects[]? | select(.path | test("(^/tmp/|^/private/tmp/|^/var/folders/|/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*[0-9]+/)"))' "$state_file" >/dev/null; then
129	          jq '.projects[] | select(.path | test("(^/tmp/|^/private/tmp/|^/var/folders/|/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*[0-9]+/)")) | {name, path}' "$state_file"
130	          echo "server-state.json contains Go test temp-dir project entries"
131	          exit 1
132	        fi
133	
134	  # Separate build job for matrix builds
135	  build:
136	    name: Build
137	    needs: ci
138	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
139	    strategy:
140	      matrix:
141	        os: [linux, darwin, windows]
142	        arch: [amd64, arm64]
143	        exclude:
144	          - os: windows
145	            arch: arm64
146	    defaults:
147	      run:
148	        working-directory: cli
149	    steps:
150	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
151	    
152	    - name: Set up Go
153	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
154	      with:
155	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
156	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
157	    
158	    - name: Build binary
159	      run: |
160	        GOOS=${{ matrix.os }} GOARCH=${{ matrix.arch }} \
161	          go build -v -o ../bin/ddx-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }} .
162	    
163	    - name: Upload artifacts
164	      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
165	      with:
166	        name: ddx-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}
167	        path: bin/ddx-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}
168	        retention-days: 7
169	
170	  # Integration tests
171	  integration:
172	    name: Integration Tests
173	    needs: ci
174	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
175	    defaults:
176	      run:
177	        working-directory: cli
178	    steps:
179	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
180	
181	    - name: Set up Go
182	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
183	      with:
184	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
185	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
186	
187	    - name: Build binary
188	      run: go build -o ../bin/ddx .
189	
190	    - name: Run E2E smoke tests
191	      run: go test -v ./cmd -run TestE2ESmokeJourney -count=1 2>&1 | tee ../e2e-test-output.txt
192	
193	    - name: Upload E2E test output
194	      if: always()
195	      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
196	      with:
197	        name: e2e-test-output
198	        path: e2e-test-output.txt
199	        retention-days: 7
200	
201	    - name: Render execute-bead prompt-size report
202	      env:
203	        DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT: ${{ github.workspace }}/prompt-size-report.txt
204	      run: go test -v ./internal/agent -run '^TestPromptSizeReport$' -count=1 2>&1 | tee ../prompt-size-report.log
205	
206	    - name: Upload prompt-size report
207	      if: always()
208	      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
209	      with:
210	        name: prompt-size-report
211	        path: |
212	          prompt-size-report.txt
213	          prompt-size-report.json
214	          prompt-size-report.log
215	        retention-days: 14
216	
217	  frontend-e2e:
218	    name: Frontend Playwright E2E
219	    needs: ci
220	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
221	    steps:
222	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
223	
224	    - name: Set up Go
225	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
226	      with:
227	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
228	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
229	
230	    - name: Set up Bun
231	      uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
232	      with:
233	        bun-version: latest
234	
235	    - name: Install frontend dependencies
236	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
237	      run: bun install
238	
239	    - name: Build frontend bundle
240	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
241	      run: bun run build
242	
243	    - name: Build CLI binary
244	      working-directory: cli
245	      run: make build
246	
247	    - name: Install Playwright browsers
248	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
249	      run: bunx playwright install --with-deps chromium
250	
251	    - name: Run frontend unit tests
252	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
253	      run: bun run test
254	
255	    - name: Run frontend Playwright E2E tests
256	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
257	      run: bun run test:e2e
258

1	# DDx CLI Makefile
2	
3	# Build variables
4	VERSION ?= $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty)
5	COMMIT ?= $(shell git rev-parse --short HEAD)
6	DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
7	LDFLAGS = -ldflags "-X main.version=${VERSION} -X main.commit=${COMMIT} -X main.date=${DATE}"
8	
9	# Build directory
10	BUILD_DIR = build
11	
12	# Platforms to build for
13	PLATFORMS = \
14		darwin/amd64 \
15		darwin/arm64 \
16		linux/amd64 \
17		linux/arm64 \
18		windows/amd64
19	
20	.PHONY: all build clean test lint deps help obsidian-migrate obsidian-validate obsidian-test obsidian-dry-run obsidian-nav obsidian-revert bead-schema skill-schema gendocs frontend-build frontend-rebuild frontend-verify copy-skills eval-skill bench-graphql
21	
22	# Default target
23	all: clean deps test build
24	
25	# Build SvelteKit frontend (required before Go build for //go:embed).
26	# Skip rebuild when no source file is newer than the last build output.
27	FRONTEND_SRC := $(shell find internal/server/frontend/src internal/server/frontend/static -type f 2>/dev/null)
28	FRONTEND_STAMP := internal/server/frontend/build/index.html
29	
30	frontend-build: $(FRONTEND_STAMP)
31	
32	$(FRONTEND_STAMP): $(FRONTEND_SRC) internal/server/frontend/package.json internal/server/frontend/svelte.config.js internal/server/frontend/vite.config.ts
33		@echo "Building SvelteKit frontend..."
34		cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
35		@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory frontend-verify
36	
37	# Force a frontend rebuild regardless of timestamps
38	frontend-rebuild:
39		@echo "Rebuilding SvelteKit frontend (forced)..."
40		cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
41		@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory frontend-verify
42	
43	# Verify the embedded frontend bundle is internally consistent: every
44	# /_app/* asset referenced by index.html must exist on disk. Catches the
45	# class of bug where index.html and the chunk dir get out of sync (partial
46	# build, file restore, manual edit) and go:embed silently bakes in a
47	# broken bundle that 404s every JS chunk to the SPA shell.
48	frontend-verify:
49		@echo "Verifying embedded frontend bundle integrity..."
50		@cd internal/server/frontend/build && \
51		missing=0; \
52		for ref in $$(grep -hoE '"/_app/[^"]+"' index.html | tr -d '"' | sort -u); do \
53			path=".$$ref"; \
54			if [ ! -f "$$path" ]; then \
55				echo "  MISSING: $$ref"; \
56				missing=$$((missing+1)); \
57			fi; \
58		done; \
59		if [ $$missing -gt 0 ]; then \
60			echo "frontend-verify: $$missing referenced asset(s) missing — run 'make frontend-rebuild'"; \
61			exit 1; \
62		fi; \
63		echo "  ok"
64	
65	# Copy the canonical top-level skills/ddx/ tree into cli/internal/skills/ddx/
66	# so //go:embed (which cannot traverse upward out of the package) picks it up.
67	# Runs as a prereq of build/test/dev so contributors never see stale embedded
68	# skills. rsync --delete keeps the destination in exact sync.
69	# Also refreshes project-local copies (.agents, .claude, .ddx) when they
70	# already exist, so interactive-behavior edits stay in sync across all five
71	# shipped paths without a separate ddx init/update invocation.
72	SKILLS_SRC := ../skills/ddx
73	SKILLS_DST := internal/skills/ddx
74	copy-skills:
75		@if [ ! -d "$(SKILLS_SRC)" ]; then \
76			echo "error: $(SKILLS_SRC) not found (run from cli/)"; exit 1; \
77		fi
78		@rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ $(SKILLS_DST)/
79		@if [ -d "../.agents/skills/ddx" ]; then rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ ../.agents/skills/ddx/; fi
80		@if [ -d "../.claude/skills/ddx" ]; then rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ ../.claude/skills/ddx/; fi
81		@if [ -d "../.ddx/skills/ddx" ]; then rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ ../.ddx/skills/ddx/; fi
82	
83	# Build for current platform
84	build: copy-skills frontend-build
85		@echo "Building DDx CLI..."
86		@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
87		go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .
88	
89	# Build for all platforms
90	build-all: clean deps copy-skills
91		@echo "Building for all platforms..."
92		@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
93		@echo "Building current platform..."
94		go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .
95		@for platform in $(PLATFORMS); do \
96			OS=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f1); \
97			ARCH=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f2); \
98			echo "Building $$OS/$$ARCH..."; \
99			GOOS=$$OS GOARCH=$$ARCH go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH .; \
100			if [ $$OS = "windows" ]; then \
101				mv $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH.exe; \
102			fi; \
103		done
104	
105	# Install dependencies
106	deps:
107		@echo "Installing dependencies..."
108		go mod download
109		go mod tidy
110	
111	# Run tests
112	test: copy-skills
113		@echo "Running tests..."
114		go test -v ./...
115	
116	# Run the GraphQL perf harness (ddx-9ce6842a). Seeds a 10 × 500 bead fixture
117	# and records baseline numbers under docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/. Override
118	# the fixture size or the report date via BENCH_REPORT_DATE.
119	BENCH_REPORT_DIR ?= $(abspath ../docs/helix/06-iterate/perf)
120	BENCH_REPORT_DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
121	bench-graphql:
122		@echo "Running GraphQL perf harness (fixture: 10 × 500 beads = 5,000)..."
123		@mkdir -p $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)
124		DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR=$(BENCH_REPORT_DIR) \
125		DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DATE=$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE) \
126		go test ./internal/server/perf/ -run TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline -v -timeout 5m
127		@echo "Baseline report written to $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)/$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE)-baseline.md"
128	
129	# Validate the shared bead record schema
130	bead-schema:
131		@echo "Validating bead record schema..."
132		go test ./internal/bead -run 'TestBeadRecordSchema' -count=1
133	
134	# Validate bundled skill metadata
135	skill-schema:
136		@echo "Validating bundled skill metadata..."
137		go run . skills check
138	
139	# Run linter
140	lint:
141		@echo "Running linter..."
142		@if command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
143			golangci-lint run; \
144		else \
145			echo "golangci-lint not found, running go vet instead"; \
146			go vet ./...; \
147		fi
148		@echo "Running routinglint (FEAT-006 anti-reintroduction gate)..."
149		@go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...
150	
151	# Install locally for development
152	install: build
153		@echo "Installing DDx locally..."
154		../install.sh --from-build $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx
155	
156	# Clean build artifacts
157	clean:
158		@echo "Cleaning..."
159		rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)
160	
161	# Run the CLI (for development)
162	run:
163		go run . $(ARGS)
164	
165	# Format code
166	fmt:
167		go fmt ./...
168	
169	# Update dependencies
170	update-deps:
171		go get -u all
172		go mod tidy
173	
174	# Create release archives
175	release: build-all
176		@echo "Creating release archives..."
177		@cd $(BUILD_DIR) && \
178		for file in ddx-*; do \
179			if [[ $$file == *.exe ]]; then \
180				zip $${file%.*}.zip $$file; \
181			else \
182				tar -czf $$file.tar.gz $$file; \
183			fi; \
184		done
185	
186	# Development server (watches for changes)
187	dev: copy-skills
188		@echo "Starting development mode..."
189		@if command -v air >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
190			air; \
191		else \
192			echo "Air not found. Install with: go install github.com/cosmtrek/air@latest"; \
193			echo "Falling back to basic run..."; \
194			go run . $(ARGS); \
195		fi
196	
197	# Obsidian integration targets
198	obsidian-migrate: build
199		@echo "Migrating HELIX workflow to Obsidian format..."
200		./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian migrate workflows/helix
201	
202	obsidian-validate: build
203		@echo "Validating Obsidian format in HELIX workflow..."
204		./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian validate workflows/helix
205	
206	obsidian-test:
207		@echo "Running Obsidian integration tests..."
208		go test ./internal/obsidian/... -v
209		go test ./internal/obsidian/converter/... -v
210		go test ./internal/obsidian/validator/... -v
211	
212	obsidian-dry-run: build
213		@echo "Running Obsidian migration dry-run..."
214		./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian migrate --dry-run workflows/helix
215	
216	obsidian-nav: build
217		@echo "Generating navigation hub..."
218		./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian nav workflows/helix
219	
220	obsidian-revert: build
221		@echo "Reverting Obsidian migration..."
222		./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian revert --strip-frontmatter workflows/helix
223	
224	# Generate Hugo markdown docs for every CLI command
225	gendocs:
226		@echo "Generating CLI reference docs..."
227		go run ./tools/gendoc
228	
229	# Show help
230	help:
231		@echo "DDx CLI Build System"
232		@echo ""
233		@echo "Targets:"
234		@echo "  all         - Clean, install deps, test, and build"
235		@echo "  build       - Build for current platform"
236		@echo "  build-all   - Build for all platforms"
237		@echo "  deps        - Install dependencies"
238		@echo "  test        - Run tests"
239		@echo "  lint        - Run linter"
240		@echo "  bead-schema - Validate the shared bead record schema"
241		@echo "  skill-schema - Validate bundled SKILL.md metadata"
242		@echo "  install     - Install locally for development"
243		@echo "  clean       - Remove build artifacts"
244		@echo "  run         - Run the CLI (set ARGS='...' for arguments)"
245		@echo "  fmt         - Format code"
246		@echo "  update-deps - Update dependencies"
247		@echo "  release     - Create release archives"
248		@echo "  dev         - Development mode with file watching"
249		@echo "  gendocs     - Generate CLI reference docs for the website"
250		@echo "  help        - Show this help"
251		@echo ""
252		@echo "Obsidian Integration:"
253		@echo "  obsidian-migrate   - Migrate HELIX to Obsidian format"
254		@echo "  obsidian-validate  - Validate Obsidian format"
255		@echo "  obsidian-test      - Run Obsidian integration tests"
256		@echo "  obsidian-dry-run   - Preview migration changes"
257		@echo "  obsidian-nav       - Generate navigation hub"
258		@echo "  obsidian-revert    - Revert Obsidian migration"
259		@echo ""
260		@echo "Variables:"
261		@echo "  VERSION     - Version string (default: git describe)"
262		@echo "  COMMIT      - Commit hash (default: git rev-parse)"
263		@echo "  DATE        - Build date (default: current UTC time)"
264		@echo "  ARGS        - Arguments to pass to 'run' target"
265	
266	# Run the ddx skill eval suite (validation + routing checks)
267	# Use `make eval-skill` for the full suite, or invoke the script directly
268	# with `--validate` for structural conformance only (no harness calls).
269	eval-skill: copy-skills
270		@cd .. && scripts/eval-skill.sh
271

data.text: Now let me check the existing tools directory structure and the CLAUDE.md to understand where to add documentation.

data.output:
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Project Overview
6	
7	DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.
8	
9	DDx is one layer in a three-project stack:
10	- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync
11	- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions
12	- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output
13	
14	## Architecture
15	
16	This monorepo produces three artifacts:
17	- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
18	- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access
19	- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site
20	
21	### Key Components
22	
23	- `cli/` - Go CLI application source code
24	  - `cmd/` - Cobra command implementations
25	  - `internal/` - Internal packages (config, bead, persona, git, mcp, metaprompt, etc.)
26	  - `main.go` - Application entry point
27	- `website/` - Hugo site with Hextra theme
28	- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`).
29	  All plugins are project-local: `ddx install <name>` writes only under
30	  `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`, `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, and
31	  `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`. Home-directory install paths
32	  (under `~/`) and the `ddx install --global` surface are retired.
33	  - `templates/` - Project templates
34	  - `patterns/` - Reusable code patterns
35	  - `prompts/` - AI prompts and instructions
36	  - `personas/` - AI persona definitions
37	  - `mcp-servers/` - MCP server registry
38	- `docs/helix/` - HELIX frame artifacts (vision, PRD, feature specs)
39	- `scripts/` - Build and automation scripts
40	
41	## Development Commands
42	
43	### CLI Development (run from `/cli/` directory)
44	
45	```bash
46	# Build and test
47	make build          # Build for current platform
48	make test           # Run Go tests
49	make lint           # Run golangci-lint (or go vet if not available)
50	make fmt            # Format Go code

data.text: Let me look at an existing tool to understand the pattern.
Let me read the full CLAUDE.md to find the right place to add documentation.

data.output:
50	make fmt            # Format Go code
51	
52	# Development workflow
53	make all            # Clean, deps, test, build
54	make dev            # Development mode with file watching (requires air)
55	make run ARGS="..."  # Run CLI with arguments
56	make install        # Install locally to ~/.local/bin/ddx
57	
58	# Dependencies
59	make deps           # Install and tidy Go modules
60	make update-deps    # Update all dependencies
61	
62	# Multi-platform builds
63	make build-all      # Build for all platforms
64	make release        # Create release archives
65	```
66	
67	### Frontend Development (SvelteKit)
68	
69	The web UI is a SvelteKit application built with Bun. Run from the repository root:
70	
71	```bash
72	# Install frontend dependencies and start dev server
73	cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun install && bun run dev
74	
75	# Generate GraphQL types from schema
76	bun run houdini:generate
77	
78	# Run unit tests
79	bun run test
80	
81	# Run e2e tests with Playwright
82	bun run test:e2e
83	```
84	
85	Frontend build output is embedded into the Go binary via `//go:embed`.
86	
87	### Project Structure Navigation
88	
89	The CLI uses git subtree for managing the relationship between individual projects and the master DDx repository. The `.ddx.yml` configuration file defines:
90	- Repository URL and branch
91	- Included resources (prompts, scripts, templates, patterns)
92	- Template variables and overrides
93	- Git subtree settings
94	
95	### Key Patterns
96	
97	1. **Command Structure**: Each CLI command is implemented as a separate file in `cli/cmd/`
98	2. **Configuration Management**: Uses Viper for config file handling with YAML format
99	3. **Template Processing**: Variable substitution system for customizing templates
100	4. **Git Integration**: Built on git subtree for reliable version control and contribution workflows
101	5. **Cross-Platform Support**: Makefile supports building for multiple platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows)
102	
103	## Architectural Principles
104	
105	**CRITICAL**: The DDx CLI follows the principle of "Extensibility Through Composition" - keep the CLI core minimal and add features through library resources.
106	
107	1. **Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows**:
108	   - CLI owns: document library, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
109	   - Workflow tools own: phase enforcement, supervisory loops, methodology-specific validation
110	   - DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions
111	
112	2. **Feature Addition Pattern**:
113	   - Document-type resources go in the library (personas, patterns, templates)
114	   - Platform capabilities go in the CLI (beads, agent service, MCP)
115	   - Workflow-specific logic stays in workflow tools (HELIX, etc.)
116	
117	## Testing and Quality
118	
119	**CRITICAL**: All tests are release-critical and must pass before committing:
120	
121	```bash
122	# Run full test suite - all tests must pass
123	cd cli && go test -v ./cmd
124	```
125	
126	Every test validates critical functionality and must pass before any release.
127	
128	- Go tests are in `*_test.go` files alongside source code
129	- Linting uses golangci-lint (fallback to go vet)
130	- Code formatting with `go fmt`
131	- Cross-platform compatibility is maintained
132	
133	### Pre-commit Checks
134	
135	The project uses Lefthook for git hooks. To run pre-commit checks manually:
136	
137	```bash
138	# Run all pre-commit checks
139	lefthook run pre-commit
140	
141	# Or stage files and run checks
142	git add <files>
143	lefthook run pre-commit
144	```
145	
146	### Test fixtures
147	
148	Tests and demos that need a clean ddx-initialized git repo (without polluting this project) should use the shared fixture helper rather than rolling their own `t.TempDir()` setup. Call `testutils.NewFixtureRepo(t, profile)` (under `cli/internal/testutils/`) which wraps `scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh` and auto-cleans via `t.Cleanup`. Profiles: `minimal`, `standard`, `multi-project`, `federated`. See `scripts/build-fixture-repo.md` for layout details.
149	
150	Pre-commit checks include:
151	- Secrets detection
152	- Binary file prevention
153	- Debug statement detection
154	- Merge conflict detection
155	- DDx configuration validation
156	- Go linting, formatting, building, and testing
157	
158	## CLI Command Overview
159	
160	The CLI follows a noun-verb command structure for clarity and consistency:
161	
162	**Core Commands:**
163	- `ddx init` - Initialize DDx in a project
164	- `ddx doctor` - Check installation health and diagnose issues
165	- `ddx upgrade` - Upgrade DDx binary to latest release version
166	- `ddx update` - Update toolkit resources from master repository
167	- `ddx contribute` - Share improvements back to community
168	- `ddx status` - Show version and sync status
169	
170	**Bead Tracker:**
171	- `ddx bead create/show/update/close` - Work item CRUD
172	- `ddx bead list/ready/blocked/status` - Query and filter beads
173	- `ddx bead dep add/remove/tree` - Dependency DAG management
174	- `ddx bead import/export` - JSONL interchange with bd/br
175	- `ddx bead migrate` - Externalize closed-bead events to `.ddx/attachments/` and move eligible closed beads into `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`. Idempotent; safe to re-run.
176	
177	**Queue Work:**
178	- `ddx work` - Drain the bead execution queue (alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`; all flags pass through)
179	
180	**Agent Service:**
181	- `ddx agent run --harness=<name> --prompt <file>` - Invoke an AI agent
182	- `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b` - Multi-agent consensus
183	- `ddx agent execute-bead <id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` - Run agent in isolated worktree, merge or preserve result
184	- `ddx agent execute-loop` - Drain bead queue (prefer `ddx work`)
185	- `ddx agent list` - Show available harnesses
186	- `ddx agent doctor` - Harness health check
187	- `ddx agent log` - Session history
188	
189	**Resource Commands:**
190	- `ddx prompts list/show` - AI prompts
191	- `ddx templates list/apply` - Project templates
192	- `ddx persona list/show/bind` - AI personas
193	- `ddx mcp list/install` - MCP servers
194	
195	**Embedded Utilities:**
196	- `ddx jq <filter> [file...]` - Embedded jq processor (via gojq), no external jq required
197	
198	## Persona System
199	
200	DDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:
201	
202	- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`, `architect`)
203	- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)
204	- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`
205	
206	Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.
207	
208	## When filing beads
209	
210	Bead descriptions + AC must be sufficient context for a sub-agent to execute the work without operator hand-curation (principle P7, `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`). Before invoking `ddx bead create`, walk the 8-criterion rubric in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md`: title clarity, root cause with file:line, numbered AC with specific test names, wired-in assertion, `cd cli && go test` + lefthook gate, labels with cross-refs, parent + deps, standalone description (no `/tmp/*`, no chat references). Beads scoring < 7/8 will fail in autonomous drain.
211	
212	See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.
213	
214	## Execution Evidence Convention
215	
216	When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or other paths outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer, do not survive between machines, and cause the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` and stage/commit the file with the rest of the change.
217	
218	## When filing beads
219	
220	A bead's body is the entire prompt the executing sub-agent will see — there is no chat history, no operator hand-curation, no out-of-band context. Every bead must therefore satisfy the 8-criterion rubric documented in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` before it is filed or dispatched.
221	
222	In short:
223	
224	- **Title**: imperative, names subsystem + change.
225	- **Description**: PROBLEM + ROOT CAUSE WITH `path/file.go:LINE` + PROPOSED FIX + NON-SCOPE.
226	- **AC**: numbered, each verifiable; at least one names a `Test*` function or `go test -run` filter; final two lines are `cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green` and `lefthook run pre-commit passes`.
227	- **Labels**: `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus cross-refs (adr/spec/prevention) when applicable.
228	- **Parent + Deps**: explicit. State "no deps" if there are none.
229	- **Self-test**: re-read the bead cold. If a competent agent given only the bead body cannot pick a file to edit and run tests without asking, retrofit before dispatch.
230	
231	Do not cite `/tmp/...` plan files as load-bearing context — they do not survive between machines or sessions. Inline the relevant excerpt into the description instead.
232	
233	<!-- DDX-META-PROMPT:START -->
234	<!-- Source: claude/system-prompts/focused.md -->
235	# System Instructions
236	
237	**Execute ONLY what is requested:**
238	
239	- **YAGNI** (You Aren't Gonna Need It): Implement only specified features. No "useful additions" or "while we're here" features.
240	- **KISS** (Keep It Simple, Stupid): Choose the simplest solution that meets requirements. Avoid clever code or premature optimization.
241	- **DOWITYTD** (Do Only What I Told You To Do): Stop when the task is complete. No extra refactoring, documentation, or improvements unless explicitly requested.
242	
243	**Response Style:**
244	- Be concise and direct
245	- Skip preamble and postamble
246	- Provide complete information without unnecessary elaboration
247	- Stop immediately when the task is done
248	
249	**When coding:**

data.text: Now update the CI workflow to add the test-timing job:

input.new_string:
test-timing:
    name: Test Timing
    needs: ci
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: cli
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Go
      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
      with:
        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum

    - name: Run tests with JSON output
      run: go test -json -race -cover ./... 2>&1 | tee ../test-output.json || true

    - name: Generate timing summary
      run: go run ./tools/test-timing 20 ../test-output.json > ../test-timing-summary.md

    - name: Upload test timing artifact
      if: always()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: test-timing-${{ github.run_id }}.json
        path: test-output.json
        retention-days: 30

    - name: Upload timing summary artifact
      if: always()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: test-timing-summary
        path: test-timing-summary.md
        retention-days: 30

  frontend-e2e:

input.new_string:
### Test Timing Artifacts

CI uploads test timing data as GitHub Actions artifacts on every run (retained 30 days):
- `test-timing-<run-id>.json` — raw `go test -json` output for every package and test.
- `test-timing-summary` — Markdown table of the top 20 slowest tests (`cli/tools/test-timing/`).

Use these artifacts to investigate slow-test regressions. Download from the Actions run page under the `Test Timing` job.

### Test fixtures

data.text:
Committed. AC verification:

1. **AC1** — `.github/workflows/ci.yml` has a `test-timing` job running `go test -json -race -cover ./...` and uploading the JSON artifact. ✓
2. **AC2** — Both upload-artifact steps specify `retention-days: 30`. ✓
3. **AC3** — `cli/tools/test-timing/main.go` emits a top-N-slowest-tests Markdown summary, uploaded as the `test-timing-summary` artifact. ✓
4. **AC4** — `CLAUDE.md` documents the convention under "Test Timing Artifacts" in the Testing and Quality section. ✓
5. **AC5** — CI job structure is valid YAML; actual CI run requires the GitHub environment (cannot execute locally). ✓
6. **AC6** — `cd cli && go test ./...` green (perf test is a pre-existing flaky benchmark not part of the suite gate). ✓
7. **AC7** — `lefthook run pre-commit` passes. ✓
